what are the latests technology news?

======================================================================
TOP TECHNOLOGY NEWS - TECHNOLOGY NEWS
======================================================================
Fetched at: 2026-06-09 14:19:07
Total articles: 10
======================================================================

[1] How an e-scooter founder raised $5 million to build space data centers
    Source: TechCrunch | Published: Tue, 09 Jun 2026 12:00:00 +000
    Orbital founder Euwyn Poon built 250,000 scooters at Spin. Now he wants to launch 10,000 space data centers.
    URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/09/how-an-e-scooter-founder-raised-5-million-to-build-space-data-centers/
----------------------------------------------------------------------

[2] Evotrex raises $30M to build the RV that doesn’t need a charging station
    Source: TechCrunch | Published: Tue, 09 Jun 2026 09:00:00 +000
    The startup is one of many entering the RV space, but it's banking on a hybrid power system that can go far beyond campsites.
    URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/09/anker-backed-evotrex-raises-30m-to-build-its-off-grid-rv/
----------------------------------------------------------------------

[3] Zepto’s IPO filing reveals fast growth, bigger losses, and a valuation question nobody’s answered yet
    Source: TechCrunch | Published: Tue, 09 Jun 2026 05:07:17 +000
    Zepto's advertising revenue jumped 151%, outpacing the company's 104% growth in operating revenue.
    URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/08/zeptos-ipo-filing-reveals-fast-growth-bigger-losses-and-a-valuation-question-nobodys-answered-yet/
----------------------------------------------------------------------

[4] Why Apple’s slow-and-steady AI bet is starting to look pretty smart
    Source: TechCrunch | Published: Tue, 09 Jun 2026 01:56:48 +000
    Can Apple's new AI glow up put to bed accusations that it's losing an all-important industry race?
    URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/08/why-apples-slow-and-steady-ai-bet-is-starting-to-look-pretty-smart/
----------------------------------------------------------------------

[5] Mercor’s Brendan Foody calls out Sequoia, accusing it of ‘dual-pricing’ valuation tricks
    Source: TechCrunch | Published: Tue, 09 Jun 2026 00:45:56 +000
    Sequoia is just one of the top firms that sells same equity at two different prices.
    URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/08/mercors-brendan-foody-calls-out-sequoia-over-dual-pricing-valuation-tricks/
----------------------------------------------------------------------

[6] As OpenAI files for IPO, Sam Altman’s eye-scanning company is doing layoffs, report says
    Source: TechCrunch | Published: Mon, 08 Jun 2026 22:41:45 +000
    Tools for Humanity, Sam Altman's identity verification company, is reportedly struggling to generate revenue and will downsize its staff.
    URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/08/as-openai-files-for-ipo-sam-altmans-eye-scanning-company-is-doing-layoffs-report-says/
----------------------------------------------------------------------

[7] Apple’s WWDC AI demos looked more real after $250M false ad settlement
    Source: TechCrunch | Published: Mon, 08 Jun 2026 22:39:57 +000
    The vibe of Apple's 2026 WWDC keynote felt like a spouse proudly listing all the honey-do-list items tackled. One subtle example: the many AI demos of someone standing, phone in hand.
    URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/08/apples-wwdc-ai-demos-looked-more-real-after-250m-false-ad-settlement/
----------------------------------------------------------------------

[8] OpenAI files confidentially for IPO, following Anthropic
    Source: TechCrunch | Published: Mon, 08 Jun 2026 21:29:57 +000
    The filing comes a little more than a week after its main rival, Anthropic, also filed to go public, ramping up the race between the two AI firms.
    URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/08/following-anthropic-openai-files-confidentially-for-ipo/
----------------------------------------------------------------------

[9] Apple plays catch-up at WWDC
    Source: TechCrunch | Published: Mon, 08 Jun 2026 21:15:42 +000
    Apple spent much of its WWDC keynote highlighting fixes, performance improvements, and long-requested features before unveiling its upgraded AI-powered Siri, signaling that the company wants users to ...
    URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/08/apple-plays-catch-up-at-wwdc/
----------------------------------------------------------------------

[10] Apple bets cheaper AI will woo small developers
    Source: TechCrunch | Published: Mon, 08 Jun 2026 20:53:55 +000
    As AI experimentation grows more expensive, Apple is waiving cloud API costs for developers with fewer than 2 million first-time App Store downloads.
    URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/08/apple-bets-cheaper-ai-will-woo-small-developers/
----------------------------------------------------------------------
history.name: get-tech-news

history.content:
Here are the latest technology news headlines:

## **TOP TECHNOLOGY NEWS**

**1. How an e-scooter founder raised $5 million to build space data centers**  
*Source: TechCrunch | Orbital founder Euwyn Poon built 250,000 scooters at Spin. Now he wants to launch 10,000 space data centers.*

**2. Evotrex raises $30M to build the RV that doesn't need a charging station**  
*Source: TechCrunch | The startup is one of many entering the RV space, but it's banking on a hybrid power system that can go far beyond campsites.*

**3. Zepto's IPO filing reveals fast growth, bigger losses, and a valuation question nobody's answered yet**  
*Source: TechCrunch | Zepto's advertising revenue jumped 151%, outpacing the company's 104% growth in operating revenue.*

**4. Why Apple's slow-and-steady AI bet is starting to look pretty smart**  
*Source: TechCrunch | Can Apple's new AI glow up put to bed accusations that it's losing an all-important industry race?*

**5. Mercor's Brendan Foody calls out Sequoia, accusing it of 'dual-pricing' valuation tricks**  
*Source: TechCrunch | Sequoia is just one of the top firms that sells same equity at two different prices.*

**6. As OpenAI files for IPO, Sam Altman's eye-scanning company is doing layoffs, report says**  
*Source: TechCrunch | Tools for Humanity, Sam Altman's identity verification company, is reportedly struggling to generate revenue and will downsize its staff.*

**7. Apple's WWDC AI demos looked more real after $250M false ad settlement**  
*Source: TechCrunch | The vibe of Apple's 2026 WWDC keynote felt like a spouse proudly listing all the honey-do-list items tackled.*

**8. OpenAI files confidentially for IPO, following Anthropic**  
*Source: TechCrunch | The filing comes a little more than a week after its main rival, Anthropic, also filed to go public.*

**9. Apple plays catch-up at WWDC**  
*Source: TechCrunch | Apple spent much of its WWDC keynote highlighting fixes, performance improvements, and long-requested features before unveiling its upgraded AI-powered Siri.*

**10. Apple bets cheaper AI will woo small developers**  
*Source: TechCrunch | As AI experimentation grows more expensive, Apple is waiving cloud API costs for developers with fewer than 2 million first-time App Store downloads.*

Would you like more details on any of these stories?
